audit_items.proof_trace: `timeout_manager.cc` is a thin delegation wrapper; the only pointer dereferenced in this file is `_imp`, a `shared_ptr<TimeoutManagerImpl>` initialized via `reset(new TimeoutManagerImpl())` as the first statement of the constructor — `new` either returns a valid non-null pointer or throws, so `_imp` is never null at any dereference site. Copy/move are disabled by `SOFA_PBRPC_DISALLOW_EVIL_CONSTRUCTORS`, and C++ guarantees the destructor is not called on a partially-constructed object. The `callback` parameter is passed through to the implementation but is not dereferenced in this file.
